target.name: Physical Intelligence
target.founder: Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, Brian Ichter

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Black Forest Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $3.25B

data.narrative:
Physical Intelligence, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, and Brian Ichter, carries a current post-money valuation of $5.6B following $600M+ in total fundraising. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 9 companies (1 public: Palantir; 8 private) operating in the same sector and comparable or adjacent funding stages, including Figure AI, Reflection AI, Applied Intuition, Skild AI, and Together AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, anchoring to the $5.6B last post-money and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, implies $10.6B. The peer-median method across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $14.5B, with a range spanning $3.2B to $39.0B. These approaches reflect the substantial variance present in early-stage AI valuations.

Triangulating the two methods produces a valuation range of $9.0B to $16.7B, with a median of $12.6B—approximately 2.3x the current $5.6B valuation. The gap between stage-progression ($10.6B) and peer-median ($14.5B) reflects material uncertainty in how the market is pricing comparable-stage AI companies. Key uncertainties include revenue traction and product-market validation specifics, which are not disclosed in available data, and the extreme range observed in peer valuations, suggesting heterogeneous risk assessment across this cohort.
